title: Observability, Capacity, Flow Control, Alarms, and SLOs · Contract Defense
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript messaging decisions for observability, capacity, flow control, alarms, and slos and requires the smallest defensible messaging telemetry, capacity, and SLO evidence board with changed-case evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for messaging telemetry, capacity, and SLO evidence board. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Instrument publish, confirm, return, route, deliver, process, ack, retry, dead-letter, and effect phases with bounded identities and causal links.
Interpret ready, unacknowledged, total, ingress, delivery, ack, redelivery, consumers, consumer capacity, connections, channels, and node resource metrics together.
Model arrival rate, service rate, concurrency, oldest age, burst, retry amplification, byte volume, and recovery time for capacity decisions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A quiz messaging team handles case 9418 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine backlog state, oldest age, SLO, and resource alarms into bounded operations action; prove topology rollback before deleting the old binding; then add one subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Instrument publish, confirm, return, route, deliver, process, ack, retry, dead-letter, and effect phases with bounded identities and causal links.
